# RoamCode

> A local-first control plane for the real Claude Code or Codex TUI across your machines. RoamCode runs each coding-agent CLI on a Node with its existing provider login, keeps every Session alive in tmux, and lets any authorized browser operate it without translating the terminal into a chat. MIT-licensed.

Execution, source code, provider credentials, and terminal state remain on the user's Nodes. Remote access can use direct HTTPS, a self-hosted relay, or the optional outbound-only end-to-end encrypted relay. The relay routes ciphertext and minimum routing metadata rather than terminal plaintext. macOS and Linux; Windows via WSL2.

The canonical product navigation is Sessions, Automations, and Agents. Agents is Node-first; an Agent is a concrete Claude Code, Codex, or installed adapter runtime on one Node. Every Automation Run creates a new real Session on one exact Node, runtime, and working directory.

## Docs

- [README — what it is, quickstart, security model, env vars](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode#readme)
- Canonical product model: see `docs/product-model.md` in the repository.
- [Windows via WSL2 guide](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/blob/main/docs/windows-wsl.md)
- [Troubleshooting](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting.md)
- [Security policy](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/blob/main/SECURITY.md)
- [Contributing](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## Install

- One command, on the machine that has the selected CLI (`claude` or `codex`) installed and signed in: `curl -fsSL https://roamcode.ai/install | bash`
- [Repository](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode)
- [Discussions](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/discussions)
